inline_comments: Validate Twilio webhook signature using HMAC-SHA1. Twilio signs requests by concatenating the URL with sorted POST params, then computing HMAC-SHA1 with the auth token. @see https://www.twilio.com/docs/usage/webhooks/webhooks-security
HMAC-SHA1 with auth token, then base64 encode
Use timing-safe comparison to prevent timing attacks
Timing-safe string comparison to prevent timing attacks.
Still do comparison to maintain constant time
Configuration for secure URL reconstruction.

Whitelist of allowed hostnames. If provided, only these hosts will be accepted from forwarding headers. This prevents host header injection attacks. SECURITY: You must provide this OR set trustForwardingHeaders=true to use X-Forwarded-Host headers. Without either, forwarding headers are ignored.
Explicitly trust X-Forwarded-* headers without a whitelist. WARNING: Only set this to true if you trust your proxy configuration and understand the security implications. @default false
List of trusted proxy IP addresses. X-Forwarded-* headers will only be trusted if the request comes from one of these IPs. Requires remoteIP to be set for validation.
The IP address of the incoming request (for proxy validation).
Validate that a hostname matches RFC 1123 format. Prevents injection of malformed hostnames.
RFC 1123 hostname: alphanumeric, hyphens, dots
Also allow ngrok/tunnel subdomains
Safely extract hostname from a host header value. Handles IPv6 addresses and prevents injection via malformed values.
Handle IPv6 addresses: [::1]:8080
Handle IPv4/domain with optional port
Check for @ which could indicate user info injection attempt
Validate the extracted hostname

Reconstruct the public webhook URL from request headers. SECURITY: This function validates host headers to prevent host header injection attacks. When using forwarding headers (X-Forwarded-Host, etc.), always provide allowedHosts to whitelist valid hostnames. When behind a reverse proxy (Tailscale, nginx, ngrok), the original URL used by Twilio differs from the local request URL. We use standard forwarding headers to reconstruct it. Priority order: 1. X-Forwarded-Proto + X-Forwarded-Host (standard proxy headers) 2. X-Original-Host (nginx) 3. Ngrok-Forwarded-Host (ngrok specific) 4. Host header (direct connection)
SECURITY: Only trust forwarding headers if explicitly configured.
Either allowedHosts must be set (for whitelist validation) or
trustForwardingHeaders must be true (explicit opt-in to trust).
Also check trusted proxy IPs if configured
Only trust forwarding headers if: (has whitelist OR explicitly trusted) AND from trusted proxy
Determine protocol - only trust X-Forwarded-Proto from trusted proxies
Determine host - with security validation
Try forwarding headers in priority order
Fallback to Host header if no valid forwarding header found
Last resort: try to extract from ctx.url
URL parsing failed - use empty string (will result in invalid URL)
Extract path from the context URL (fallback to "/" on parse failure)
URL parsing failed
Result of Twilio webhook verification with detailed info.
Verify Telnyx webhook signature using Ed25519. Telnyx signs `timestamp|payload` and provides: - `telnyx-signature-ed25519` (Base64 signature) - `telnyx-timestamp` (Unix seconds)
Verify Twilio webhook with full context and detailed result.

Allow ngrok free tier compatibility mode (loopback only). IMPORTANT: This does NOT bypass signature verification. It only enables trusting forwarded headers on loopback so we can reconstruct the public ngrok URL that Twilio used for signing.
Whitelist of allowed hostnames for host header validation. Prevents host header injection attacks.
Explicitly trust X-Forwarded-* headers without a whitelist. WARNING: Only enable if you trust your proxy configuration. @default false
List of trusted proxy IP addresses. X-Forwarded-* headers will only be trusted from these IPs.
The remote IP address of the request (for proxy validation).
Allow skipping verification for development/testing
Reconstruct the URL Twilio used
Parse the body as URL-encoded params
Twilio webhook signatures can differ in whether port is included.
Retry a small, deterministic set of URL variants before failing closed.
ignore invalid publicUrl; primary verification already used best effort
Check if this is ngrok free tier - the URL might have different format
Plivo webhook verification
Result of Plivo webhook verification with detailed info.

Verify Plivo webhooks using V3 signature if present; fall back to V2. Header names (case-insensitive; Node provides lower-case keys): - V3: X-Plivo-Signature-V3 / X-Plivo-Signature-V3-Nonce - V2: X-Plivo-Signature-V2 / X-Plivo-Signature-V2-Nonce
Whitelist of allowed hostnames for host header validation. Prevents host header injection attacks.
Explicitly trust X-Forwarded-* headers without a whitelist. WARNING: Only enable if you trust your proxy configuration. @default false
List of trusted proxy IP addresses. X-Forwarded-* headers will only be trusted from these IPs.
The remote IP address of the request (for proxy validation).
